title: The Wolf and the Crane
lines: A Wolf had a bone stuck in his throat that he could not remove.
He offered a reward to any animal who could remove it.
A Crane, though afraid, agreed to try.
The Wolf opened his mouth, and the Crane thrust her long bill in and drew out the bone.
"Now pay me the reward," said the Crane.
"Is it not enough that I do not bite off your head?"
"You were lucky to get out of my mouth alive."
moral: It is dangerous to help the wicked.
